checks.a_analysis: The text content in the output faithfully follows the source PDF. I compared the specific phrasing in bulleted lists and paragraphs, such as 'Total of 52 response collected' and 'Justification of projects to HUD.', and found that they match the source exactly, including minor grammatical oddities. No evidence of fabricated or hallucinated body text was found in the sections provided.

The contact information on page 16 of the source PDF contains three email addresses: DABrannan@cabarruscounty.us, anna.verrill@nc.gov, and tim.johnson@nc.gov. These were compared against the final section of the JSON output and match perfectly. No other contact information (phone numbers or URLs) is present in the document to verify.

The source document consists of 16 slides/pages. The JSON output contains a placeholder indicating that content from pages 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, and 9 was omitted. This results in the complete absence of data for survey questions Q1 through Q6, including several charts and two pages of detailed qualitative responses. This is a critical omission of source material.
checks.b_evidence_source: Content from Page 4 (Q1 chart), Page 5 (Q2 chart), Page 6 (Q3 chart), Page 7 (Q4 details), Page 8 (Q5 chart), and Page 9 (Q6 details).

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains charts and bulleted lists but does not contain any formal tabular data structures. Therefore, this check is not applicable.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: For the sections included in the output (Pages 1-3 and 10-16), the text content is remarkably complete. For instance, the list of programs in Q8 matches all 23 items listed in the source columns, and the multi-paragraph question and response sections for Q9 and Q10 match the source text exactly. The wholesale omission of pages 4-9 is tracked as a C3 failure.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the output maintains a high level of fidelity to the source. Data points in the pie chart alt text on Page 10 correspond correctly to the source values. Punctuation and special formatting (like the superscript in 'March 11th' on Page 2) have been correctly preserved. Wording changes that alter meaning were not detected.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the entire output JSON against the source PDF slides. No content blocks (paragraphs, lists, headings) appear more times in the output than in the source. Text from headers/titles is appropriately used for structural navigation without unintended duplication of the body content.

The heading hierarchy is consistent. Section titles provide high-level structure, and internal headings (H2) are used for slide-level titles. Although several slides (Q8-Contacts) are grouped into a single JSON section, the internal heading levels (H2) for each slide title maintain a flat, logical structure for navigation. There are no skipped levels (e.g., H1 to H3).

The source document contains several bar and pie charts (Pages 4, 5, 6, 8, 10), but no standard data tables. The charts are correctly captured as images with descriptive alt text summarizing their data. No table structure corruption exists as no tables were present to convert.

The reading order follows the sequential slide-by-slide progression of the original PDF presentation. Multi-column lists (like the state/federal programs on Page 11) are correctly linearized into a logical top-to-bottom, left-to-right sequence.

Lists throughout the document are accurately converted. Specifically, the nested list on Page 2 ('Survey Background') is perfectly represented with appropriate nesting levels in the JSON. Numbered and bulleted items from other slides are also correctly captured as list elements.

The email contact information on Page 16 (DABrannan@cabarruscounty.us, anna.verrill@nc.gov, tim.johnson@nc.gov) is accurately captured as text in the output. There are no broken or fabricated links, and all relevant contact identifiers are preserved.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully translates the slide-based layout of the source PDF into a linear, single-page format. The visual organization of information is preserved, with slide titles becoming section headings and bulleted content following accordingly. A user can easily find the same information in the same relative order as the original document.
The source document does not contain any data tables. All information is presented as text, lists, or graphical charts. Therefore, no tables are broken or misrendered.

Multiple meaningful graphical charts from the source PDF are either broken or missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the horizontal bar charts for Q2 and Q3 are rendered as illegible solid black rectangles. Furthermore, the bar chart for Q5 and the pie chart for Q7 are entirely missing from the HTML document, leaving their respective headings with no data content beneath them.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 5 shows a bar chart for Q2; Page 6 shows a bar chart for Q3; Page 8 shows a bar chart for Q5; Page 10 shows a pie chart for Q7.
checks.b_evidence_output: Under the headings for Q2 and Q3, there are solid black rectangles. Under the headings for Q5 and Q7, no image or content is rendered.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections Q2, Q3, Q5, and Q7 in the HTML document.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of repeated content blocks, paragraphs, or headers in the HTML output. Each section of the source PDF is represented once in the resulting HTML.
